counterexample_family: truth-table/singleton hardwiring witness for gapPartialMCSP_Language (Probe 2 of the falsifiability audit)
structural_pattern: FixedParamsRoute paired with OverbroadUniformProvenance reconstructs the refuted FormulaSupportRestrictionBoundsPartial route via the Probe 8a leak (fixedParams_entails_old_under_uniformProvenance)
why_generalizes: Any candidate provenance Π that implies OverbroadUniformProvenance for the same ac0 is automatically dead by composition with NoGo_FixedParamsRoute_with_OverbroadUniformProvenance; FP-3/FP-4 must therefore work with provenance shapes strictly weaker than OverbroadUniformProvenance.

counterexample_family: alternating-slice hardwiring (truth-table at every other length, const false elsewhere) — not constructed in the active code base, but structurally satisfies both conjuncts of FP3Attempt.InSupportFunctionalDiversity
notes: FP-3 actual self-attack caveat. The candidate FP3Attempt.InSupportFunctionalDiversity formally defeats the SINGLE-SLICE truth-table hardwiring witness from Probe 2 via the abstract uniform-polyBound exclusion lemma (InSupportFunctionalDiversity_excludes_uniformPolyBound), but does NOT structurally exclude alternating-slice hardwiring shapes. This entry records the known weakness so future FP-3.b strengthening attempts can verify they close it. The candidate is NOT promoted to spec/known_guards.toml::guards.ProvenanceFilter_v1 (which stays informal); FP-4 is NOT started.
structural_pattern: alternating-slice / multi-slice hardwiring evades single-slice support diversity filter — the FP3Attempt.InSupportFunctionalDiversity candidate is satisfied by records that hardwire truth tables at infinitely many lengths AND have const false (or any sublinear-support shape) at infinitely many other lengths
why_generalizes: Any provenance filter that quantifies only the support function's range without constraining the *distribution* of hardwired vs non-hardwired lengths admits multi-slice hardwiring shapes. FP-3.b must replace the simple unboundedness conjunct with a stronger condition (e.g. unboundedness over every infinite arithmetic progression of lengths) and re-run the four-test self-attack to verify hidden payload, tautology, KnownGuards factorization, and bridge non-vacuity all still hold.

counterexample_family: log-width truth-table hardwiring: at length n, hardwire an arbitrary Boolean function on a logarithmic-width window k(n) = ceil(log2(n+1)) of variables via FormulaCircuit.rename (Fin.castLE _) (ttFormula at width k(n)); truth-table size 2^k(n) * k(n) = O((n+1) log(n+1)) is polynomially bounded, support cardinality is exactly k(n), unbounded over n and strictly less than n for n >= 1, hence both conjuncts of FP3Attempt.InSupportFunctionalDiversity are satisfied while internally the construction is exhaustive case-analysis truth-table over the log-width window — i.e. genuinely hardwiring-shaped rather than algorithmically efficient
notes: FP-3b.0 correction of NOGO-000002. The original NOGO-000002 entry incorrectly cited 'alternating-slice full-width truth-table hardwiring' as a counterexample family; that shape is provably impossible inside InPpolyFormula L because at each hardwired length m the formula has size 2^m, which violates polyBound_poly (no fixed c satisfies 2^m <= m^c + c uniformly in m). The genuine remaining caveat is log-width hardwiring (this entry), which is polynomially-bounded and therefore admissible inside InPpolyFormula L. Status is needs_review because no Lean witness has been constructed yet — the lift requires (i) Nat.log helper lemmas (logWidth n <= n, unboundedness, infinite-often n-strictly-greater-than-logWidth), (ii) (ttFormula f).size <= 6 * 2^k size bound by induction, (iii) FormulaCircuit.rename preserves size + transports support along an injective sigma, (iv) packaging into InPpolyFormula (fun _ _ => false) with polyBound n := 6*(n+1) and c = 11 for polyBound_poly, (v) a final theorem `logWidthAdversary_satisfies_diversity`. This is FP-3b.1 work and is intentionally deferred; FP-3b.0 ships only the corrected analysis and downgrade. The candidate ProvenanceFilter_v1 stays informal in spec/known_guards.toml; FP-4 is NOT started.
structural_pattern: log-width truth-table hardwiring evades support-cardinality-only diversity filter — for any function k : Nat -> Nat with k(n) unbounded, k(n) < n eventually, and 2^k(n) <= poly(n), the construction `family n := rename (Fin.castLE) (ttFormula at width k(n))` lives inside InPpolyFormula (constant-false language) and satisfies FP3Attempt.InSupportFunctionalDiversity, despite being truth-table-shaped at the log-width window
why_generalizes: Any provenance filter expressed purely in terms of the support cardinality function `n -> |support (family n)|` and not constraining the *internal structure* (gate composition, recursion depth, etc.) of the formulas in the family admits truth-table hardwiring at any sub-linear width. FP-3b.1 must therefore (a) formalize this counterexample at the InPpolyFormula level to upgrade this entry to status=formalized, then (b) replace the FP3Attempt.InSupportFunctionalDiversity shape with a stronger filter that ALSO constrains the formula structure, and re-run the four-test self-attack on the strengthened filter.
